finding: '301', '302', '307', '308' status codes and the word 'Redirects' are pure dev/boomer jargon with no plain explanation.

evidence.quote: Permanent move (301) Temporary move (302) Temporary redirect (307) Permanent redirect (308)

persona_rationale: I have literally no idea what 307 vs 308 is and I'm not going to Google it. Just give me 'moved for good' or 'moved for now.'

suggested_fix.current: Four near-identical numbered options (301/302/307/308)
suggested_fix.proposed: Default to 'Permanent move' with the number hidden, plus a one-line 'Use this when a page changed address'

finding: On a redirect validation error ('Path must be root-relative.') the form clears the entered values, and the error renders as a single top-of-form banner rather than next to the offending field.

evidence.quote: Banner 'Path must be root-relative.' above empty Old path / Destination fields
error copy: "invalid"; values preserved: false

persona_rationale: Wiping my input on a validation error is hostile — I have to retype everything to fix a single character — and a top banner doesn't tell me which field is wrong. Standard form-state preservation is table stakes.

suggested_fix.current: Form resets on validation error; single top banner
suggested_fix.proposed: Preserve all field values on error and attach the message inline to the field that failed (aria-describedby on the Old path input)

finding: The redirects table has no edit or delete control — a mistyped 301 is permanent from this UI.

evidence.quote: Redirects table has no delete/edit controls — created redirects cannot be removed from this UI.

persona_rationale: Create-only with no remove means a bad redirect (e.g. a loop or a typo'd destination) is unfixable without DB access. I'd never ship a redirects manager without delete.

suggested_fix.current: No row-level edit/delete on redirects table
suggested_fix.proposed: Add per-row Edit and Delete (with confirm) actions wired to the redirects service

finding: The public URL returned three 404s while polling immediately after publish, and the editor provided no 'live in a moment' signal or an 'Open live page' link — propagation to the public route is not instant and the UI doesn't acknowledge it.

evidence.quote: failedRequests: 404 http://localhost:3000/resources/ux-seo-review-test-1781071765035 (x3)
friction: No visible 'Open live page' link after publishing — had to type the URL manually

persona_rationale: If publish→live isn't instant, the UI must say so; otherwise a user (or a monitoring script) hits a 404 and assumes publishing failed. And making me hand-type the live URL after publishing is a missing-affordance smell.

suggested_fix.current: No live-page link post-publish; public route 404s briefly after publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Show an 'Open live page ↗' link on publish success that becomes active once the route resolves (poll/revalidate before enabling), so the link never lands on a 404

finding: The published page is just one headline, one orange button, and one FAQ — it looks empty and unfinished, but nothing warned me a page this thin shouldn't go live.

evidence.quote: Sparse live page: headline, single CTA button, one FAQ

persona_rationale: I'd be embarrassed to publish something that looks half-finished; the tool should warn me before it goes public.

suggested_fix.current: Thin page publishes with no warning
suggested_fix.proposed: Before publishing show a gentle note: 'This page is quite short. Add more content?' with 'Publish anyway' and 'Keep editing'

finding: Page web addresses are shown in tiny grey code that looks like a computer error rather than a page name.

evidence.quote: /resources/ux-seo-review-test-1781071765035

persona_rationale: Long code-looking addresses make me think something is broken; I'd rather see the plain page name.

suggested_fix.current: /resources/ux-seo-review-test-1781071765035 shown in monospace grey
suggested_fix.proposed: Lead with the friendly page title and show the address smaller and de-emphasised, or labelled 'Web address:'

finding: In the no-results state the primary 'Create page' is a full-width blue bar dwarfing the small adjacent 'Clear search' button — disproportionate button sizing.
persona_rationale: A primary button stretched to full width next to a compact secondary looks like a layout that never got spacing review.

suggested_fix.current: Oversized full-width primary next to a small secondary in the empty state
suggested_fix.proposed: Size the two empty-state buttons to a balanced pair (equal height, content-width)

finding: Archive and other row actions are one-row-at-a-time only; there is no bulk select to tidy several pages at once.

evidence.quote: Archiving is per-row only; no bulk select

persona_rationale: When I'm cleaning up old test pages, doing them one menu at a time is tedious. Not a blocker, just annoying.

suggested_fix.current: Per-row archive only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add checkboxes and a bulk archive action

finding: A floating black 'N' circle button overlaps the 'Sign out' button in the bottom-left corner, looking glitchy.

evidence.quote: black 'N' circle widget overlapping 'Sign out' label
same 'N' widget overlap on mobile

persona_rationale: It looks broken/glitchy, like two things crashed into each other. Sloppy — and on a real app that screams 'don't trust me.'

suggested_fix.current: Floating widget sits on top of the 'Sign out' label
suggested_fix.proposed: Reposition the floating widget so it never covers the sign-out control

finding: Duplicating a page produces an auto slug of random hex form (e.g. 'draft-4020bd0f') rather than a slug derived from the source page's slug.

evidence.quote: created 'Copy of {title}' with auto slug 'draft-4020bd0f' (original slug not preserved/derived)

persona_rationale: A random-hex URL for a duplicate is non-deterministic and meaningless; I'd expect 'copy-of-{source-slug}' so the URL is at least guessable and review-friendly.

suggested_fix.current: Duplicate slug = 'draft-{random-hex}'
suggested_fix.proposed: Derive the duplicate slug from the source ('copy-of-{source-slug}', de-duplicated with a numeric suffix) instead of random hex

finding: The hero CTA buttons ('STEP INSIDE', the orange CTA) carry a heavy hard-edged drop shadow / skeuomorphic offset, while the entire editor chrome uses flat pill buttons.
persona_rationale: The chrome and the canvas look like they were designed by two people with different shadow tokens.

suggested_fix.current: Skeuomorphic hard-shadow CTAs on the canvas vs flat chrome buttons
suggested_fix.proposed: Align the content CTA elevation with the flat system, or document the brand 'raised' button as an intentional content style

finding: Disclosure iconography is mixed: 'Builder support' sections use a right-triangle, the top-rail 'SEO' / 'Blocks' use a chevron, and 'Advanced SEO' uses yet another caret.

evidence.quote: Media assets / Approved claims and CTAs triangle disclosures
SEO chevron

persona_rationale: Mixed disclosure icons in the same panel are exactly the kind of inconsistency I cannot unsee.

suggested_fix.current: Three disclosure glyphs (triangle, chevron, caret)
suggested_fix.proposed: Standardise on one chevron for all expand/collapse affordances

finding: The block 'settings' gear icon is grey on some blocks and solid blue on others depending on selection, inconsistently across states.

evidence.quote: blue FAQ gear
blue Hero gear

persona_rationale: The active-state colour jumping between blocks tells me the selected style isn't systematised.

suggested_fix.current: Gear accent colour tracks selection inconsistently
suggested_fix.proposed: Tie the accent colour to selection deterministically, or keep the gear neutral and accent only the card

finding: The same toolbar button is labelled 'Save draft changes' in some states and 'Save draft' in others.

evidence.quote: Save draft changes
Save draft

persona_rationale: A button that renames itself between states is a small thing, but small inconsistent things are my whole job.

suggested_fix.current: Inconsistent button label across states ('Save draft changes' vs 'Save draft')
suggested_fix.proposed: Pick one label ('Save draft') and keep it stable

finding: A new page canvas renders entirely as light-grey placeholder text ('Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label') with no distinct 'unfilled' styling beyond low opacity, so it's unclear what is real content vs prompt.

evidence.quote: grey 'Hero headline' placeholder
New page canvas is entirely placeholder ('Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label') with no guidance

persona_rationale: Faint grey text could be real copy at low contrast or a placeholder — an undesigned empty-block state.

suggested_fix.current: Placeholder text indistinguishable from faint real copy
suggested_fix.proposed: Give placeholders a clearly non-content treatment (dashed outline / 'Add headline' affordance) so unfilled blocks read as empty states

finding: A 'Rendering…' / 'Updating' status pill appears bottom-left over the canvas while the AI FAB sits bottom-right — two floating overlays competing for the same canvas corners with different shapes.

evidence.quote: Rendering…
Rendering…

persona_rationale: Two differently shaped floating chips in opposite corners read as two teams' widgets sharing a canvas.

suggested_fix.current: Two unrelated floating overlays (status pill + AI FAB) in opposing corners
suggested_fix.proposed: Consolidate transient status into one consistent location/treatment distinct from the persistent AI FAB

finding: Block move and block-action controls are icon-only buttons rendering a bare img, and block wrapper spans carry a prohibited aria-label (aria-prohibited-attr, serious), so keyboard/AT users get poor or invalid labelling on the core editing controls.

evidence.quote: aria-prohibited-attr (serious): span[aria-label="Hero block"], span[aria-label="FAQ block"]
button "Move Page content 1": - img / button "Block actions": - img

persona_rationale: I keyboard-navigate and I notice when controls are unlabeled or carry invalid ARIA. Icon-only buttons with prohibited aria on the wrapper means the most-used editing controls don't announce cleanly when I tab to them.

suggested_fix.current: Icon-only block controls with img children; prohibited aria-label on block spans
suggested_fix.proposed: Move the aria-label to the interactive button (or a role that permits it) and ensure each move/action button has a visible-on-focus accessible name

finding: Revisions empty state uses stacked noun-phrase triggers that are hard to parse for a non-native reader

evidence.quote: Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.

persona_rationale: 'library refreshes' and 'draft restores' as nouns are ambiguous; verb-form sentences are clearer for me.

suggested_fix.current: Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.
suggested_fix.proposed: A revision is saved each time you publish the page, refresh its content libraries, or restore a draft.

finding: 'Governance comments' and 'Builder support' are abstract corporate phrases

evidence.quote: Governance comments
Builder support

persona_rationale: 'Governance' is an abstract business word; 'Internal notes' tells me plainly what the box is for.

suggested_fix.current: Governance comments / Builder support
suggested_fix.proposed: Internal notes / Content sources

finding: The editor shows two simultaneous, redundant save confirmations (a top-of-canvas banner 'Draft saved. Autosaved 3:45 PM' and a top-right pill 'Draft saved.') plus a persistent 'Rendering . .' pill bottom-left with no completion state.

evidence.quote: Banner 'Draft saved. Autosaved 3:45 PM', pill 'Draft saved.', and 'Rendering . .' pill all visible at once

persona_rationale: Two save toasts for one save and a 'Rendering…' pill that never resolves is UI noise that erodes my trust in the status indicators — if half of them are decorative, I stop believing any of them.

suggested_fix.current: Duplicate 'Draft saved' surfaces + non-terminating 'Rendering . .' pill
suggested_fix.proposed: Consolidate to a single save-status indicator and give the render/preview pill a terminal state (hide on complete or show 'Preview ready')

finding: The editor's ARIA tree ends with a bare, empty live-region <alert> node, rendered with no content.

evidence.quote: - alert

persona_rationale: An empty role=alert sitting in the DOM is the leftover scaffolding of a toast/announcer that isn't wired up — harmless visually but it tells me the live-region pattern is half-implemented, which lines up with the silent-failure findings.

suggested_fix.current: Empty role=alert live region always present
suggested_fix.proposed: Only mount the alert region when there's a message to announce, and route autosave/publish/schedule status through it so state changes are announced to screen readers

finding: Revision label is a bare lowercase verb that reads like an unfinished code string

evidence.quote: publish - Jun 10, 3:49 PM

persona_rationale: A lowercase bare 'publish' looks like a code key leaked into the UI; I cannot tell if it is a label or a bug.

suggested_fix.current: publish - Jun 10, 3:49 PM
suggested_fix.proposed: Published on 10 Jun 2026, 3:49 PM

finding: This very long page of repeated 'Picker preview / Actual resource render' pairs has no 'Back' and no explanation of why an editor would need it.

evidence.quote: Repeated 'Picker preview' / 'Actual resource render' comparison blocks

persona_rationale: A long, purpose-less page with no way back makes me feel I've broken something or gotten lost.

suggested_fix.current: Long audit page with no exit
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Back to pages' link at the top and a one-line plain explanation of who this page is for

finding: A developer-QA page ('Block preview audit') is reachable in the admin surface with no explanation of why a content editor would use it.

evidence.quote: Block preview audit — Compare every picker preview against the real resource-page render using mocked block content.

persona_rationale: Landing here as a first-timer makes me unsure whether this is part of my job; it adds doubt about what the product actually is.

suggested_fix.current: Block preview audit exposed in admin surface
suggested_fix.proposed: Hide behind a dev/internal flag, or label it 'Internal QA — block render parity'

finding: A dev/QA route (block-preview-audit, '30 block preview parity cases') is exposed in the production /admin/pages/ route tree with no 'internal only' labelling or flag gate.

evidence.quote: Page: /admin/pages/block-preview-audit (Block preview audit (dev QA)) ... 30 block preview parity cases rendered

persona_rationale: A QA-only audit page living next to real admin routes is the kind of thing that ships to prod by accident and confuses admins who stumble into it. It should be flag-gated or excluded from the prod build.

suggested_fix.current: block-preview-audit reachable under /admin/pages in all environments
suggested_fix.proposed: Gate it behind a dev/preview flag or move it out of the prod route tree, and label it 'Internal QA' if it must stay

finding: A new page opens onto an all-placeholder canvas ('Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label') with no indication that these placeholders must be replaced with real content before the page can be published.

evidence.quote: canvas shows greyed placeholders 'Hero headline' / 'Hero body copy' / 'CTA label'
New page canvas is entirely placeholder ("Hero headline", "Hero body copy", "CTA label") with no guidance that these must be completed before publishing.

persona_rationale: Placeholder text that looks like real (greyed) content is how half-baked pages get pushed live by accident. A clearer 'this is a placeholder — replace before publishing' cue would reassure me the tool guards against shipping empty pages.

suggested_fix.current: Placeholder canvas with no replace-before-publish guidance
suggested_fix.proposed: Style placeholders distinctly and add an inline hint per empty required block, e.g. 'Replace this placeholder before publishing', tied to the readiness blockers.

finding: "Template" starting point offers "No templates created" — a dead-end card for a first-time user.

evidence.quote: a "Template" option exists with "No templates created" (dead-end card for a first-time user)

persona_rationale: Offering me a template path that leads nowhere on day one is the kind of unfinished edge Webflow polished years ago; small, but it sets a "not quite done" tone.

suggested_fix.current: Empty Template option presented as a selectable card
suggested_fix.proposed: Hide the option until templates exist, or make it a "Save your first template" CTA, like Webflow only showing the gallery when populated

finding: New-page canvas is all greyed placeholder text with no guidance that fields must be filled before publishing.

evidence.quote: New page canvas is entirely placeholder ("Hero headline", "Hero body copy", "CTA label") with no guidance that these must be completed before publishing.
Editor opened with placeholder-only hero content

persona_rationale: Webflow eases a new user in; here I'm staring at grey text guessing which are required, then getting blindsided by publish blockers later. A small nudge up front would prevent the whack-a-mole downstream.

suggested_fix.current: Placeholder-only canvas with no onboarding
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a first-run hint or checklist ("Fill the hero headline to get started"), like Webflow's onboarding overlays

finding: The status choices ('Permanent move (301)', 'Temporary redirect (307)') include code numbers that confuse me and I don't know which to pick.

evidence.quote: Permanent move (301)

persona_rationale: The bracketed numbers look technical and make me doubt my choice even when the words almost make sense.

suggested_fix.current: 'Permanent move (301)' / 'Temporary redirect (307)'
suggested_fix.proposed: Default to 'Permanent move' with the recommended option marked, and hide the code numbers behind an 'Advanced' toggle

finding: The page is titled 'Redirects' which I don't understand on its own, though the subtitle helps a little.

evidence.quote: Send old page addresses to their new destinations.

persona_rationale: The word 'Redirects' alone is jargon; I rely on the plain subtitle to understand it, so the title should match that plainness.

suggested_fix.current: Heading 'Redirects'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Send old links to new pages' with 'Redirects' as a smaller subtitle

finding: The validation error 'Path must be root-relative.' is jargon and doesn't tell me how to fix it.

evidence.quote: Path must be root-relative.

persona_rationale: 'Root-relative' is developer vocabulary; a first-timer marketer doesn't know what's wrong or what a valid value looks like.

suggested_fix.current: Path must be root-relative.
suggested_fix.proposed: Paths must start with / — e.g. /resources/old-page

finding: The preview address is a long scramble that looks like a broken or suspicious link, not a safe preview to share.

evidence.quote: /resources/preview/2eSNnEXLh0znXOYIrUm2Hjk7ieSeIqY2P1z0wTAPiWw

persona_rationale: A jumbled web address looks dodgy to me; I'd be nervous to send it to anyone in case they think it's spam.

suggested_fix.current: Raw random token in the preview URL with no framing
suggested_fix.proposed: When sharing, show a short note 'This is a private preview link' so I trust it before sending
